reasons: Diggie directly reduces the value of Zetian's global stun engage.
Zetian's ultimate is strongest when it enables teamwide follow-up, while Diggie can protect the team during that window.

proof.summary: Zetian wants to use global stun and vision to start or swing fights, while Diggie can cleanse or protect nearby allies against the crowd-control payoff.
worksBestWhen: Diggie saves ultimate for Fury of the Phoenix.
Diggie is near the allies who are about to be engaged on.
Diggie's team avoids spreading so far that Time Journey cannot protect the key target.
failureCases: Zetian baits Diggie ultimate before committing global stun.
Diggie is too far from the key target.
Zetian's team engages after Diggie's protection ends.

reasons: Natalia can threaten Zetian before Zetian's global fight setup becomes valuable.
This matchup is condition-heavy because Zetian's ultimate can reveal enemy heroes during its effect, reducing surprise windows.

proof.summary: Zetian is vulnerable when caught before her teamfight setup, while Natalia can pressure her from fog; however, Zetian's global vision can reveal the timing if available.
worksBestWhen: Natalia attacks after Zetian's ultimate is down.
Natalia approaches from unwarded side angles.
Zetian's passive protection or team peel has been forced.
failureCases: Zetian uses global vision before Natalia commits.
Natalia dives into Celestial Robe protection and team peel.
Natalia's path is tracked before the ambush starts.

reasons: Hayabusa can punish Zetian as a backline utility mage when her passive protection or team peel is unavailable.
Zetian wants to cast safely over time, while mobile assassins can force her into defensive timing.

proof.summary: Zetian wants safe casting space, while Hayabusa can enter from side angles and pressure her before she controls the teamfight.
worksBestWhen: Hayabusa tracks Zetian's passive protection and nearby peel.
Zetian is isolated from tank support.
Hayabusa has shadows available to exit after the burst.
failureCases: Zetian's team holds crowd control for Hayabusa's return path.
Hayabusa dives during Zetian's protected setup.
Hayabusa cannot isolate Zetian from her frontline.

reasons: Harley can force Zetian into a short burst check before she gets extended value from Phoenix Descent and global setup.
This is strongest when Zetian's Celestial Robe cooldown has been baited or is unavailable.

proof.summary: Zetian wants to survive long enough to enable her team, while Harley can punish defensive cooldown windows with fast in-out burst.
worksBestWhen: Harley confirms Zetian's passive protection has been triggered.
Harley has escape ready after burst.
Zetian is separated from immediate peel.
failureCases: Zetian survives the burst and turns with global stun.
Harley commits before checking Zetian's protection state.

reasons: Franco can punish Zetian's backline positioning with hook and suppression if she steps too far forward for Phoenix Strike or Phoenix Descent.
This works best when Franco baits or waits out Celestial Robe's protection.

proof.summary: Zetian wants controlled casting space, while Franco can pick her off if her passive protection is unavailable or if she mispositions.
worksBestWhen: Franco attacks from fog with team follow-up.
Zetian steps forward to cast or follow up.
failureCases: Zetian's passive protection interrupts the pick attempt.
Franco hooks a frontline target instead.
Franco suppresses without enough follow-up damage.
